reason: quality review guidance — all migration phases must pass trellis-check quality gates
cross-layer consistency: frontend api-client ↔ backend /api handlers ↔ store queries; auth middleware applies to /api and /events uniformly
new api/* handlers must delegate to existing rpc/* functions (thin wrappers); do not duplicate mail/store/search logic
verify REST conventions (HTTP methods, status codes, error shape), CORS, auth middleware, SSE, composite endpoint structure
verify SQLite persistence and migrations preserved; read/write pool patterns unchanged; no schema changes introduced by API migration
verify mail sync/send/IMAP/Gmail/Outlook behavior preserved; smtp/delivery pipeline unaffected
verify OAuth login/callback flow preserved; OAUTH_REDIRECT_URL env var; token persistence unchanged
verify privacy/sanitization/tracker-blocking preserved; rendered HTML endpoint unchanged
verify credential encryption constraints; bcrypt hash for login; OAuth token encryption unchanged
verify Tantivy search behavior preserved by /api/search endpoints
verify rule matching/actions preserved under REST /api/rules CRUD
verify translate behavior preserved under /api/translate endpoints
